newDebt.title: API compatibility script does not compare runtime OpenAPI contracts
newDebt.evidence: Existing check-api-contract-compatibility.sh emits SKIPPED_NO_BASELINE with exit 0.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — oasdiff v1.21.0 (pinned) compares runtime OpenAPI vs. baseline; 5 fixtures prove engine works.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime OpenAPI generated from app and saved as openapi-current.json
oasdiff (pinned version) compares current vs. baseline
Breaking-change fixtures (path-removed, type-changed, required-field-added) all FAIL
Non-breaking fixtures (optional-field-added, new-endpoint) all PASS
Missing baseline causes CI failure (exit 1)

newDebt.title: Collection endpoints do not implement the pagination standard
newDebt.evidence: All 8 collection endpoints return ResponseEntity<List<...>> without page metadata.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — all 8 collection endpoints return PageResponse<T>; tenant-aware DB-level pagination; sort allowlists enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: 8 collection endpoints return ResponseEntity<PageResponse<...>>
Each accepts page, size, sort query parameters
totalElements and totalPages computed at DB level (no in-memory subList)
Tenant isolation tests cover pagination boundary

newDebt.title: Multiple incompatible ApiErrorResponse models remain in use
newDebt.evidence: com.sanad.platform.organization.api.ApiErrorResponse (POJO) and com.sanad.platform.shared.api.ApiErrorResponse (record) both exist.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — legacy organization.api.ApiErrorResponse POJO deleted; all consumers migrated to shared.api.ApiErrorResponse record.
acceptanceCriteria: Only com.sanad.platform.shared.api.ApiErrorResponse remains
Legacy POJO deleted, all consumers migrated
All @ApiResponse schema refs use shared model

newDebt.title: Exception responses can expose raw exception messages
newDebt.evidence: Domain handlers return ex.getMessage() verbatim; no catch-all for unexpected exceptions.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — typed exception hierarchy + GlobalExceptionHandler catch-all; raw ex.getMessage() never returned to client; actuator exclusion proven.
acceptanceCriteria: GlobalExceptionHandler has @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) catch-all
Catch-all returns SANAD-GEN-001 / 500 / generic safe detail
Internal diagnostics logged with requestId only (no stack trace to client)
Actuator endpoints remain outside ControllerAdvice scope (proven by test)

newDebt.title: Frontend API client integration is not proven
newDebt.evidence: No unified client; existing fetch calls bypass problem+json handling.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — frontend unified ApiClient handles application/problem+json + PageResponse; 15 new vitest tests cover all error paths.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web/src/lib/api/client.ts implements versioned URL, auth, requestId, timeout, abort, problem+json parsing, PageResponse parsing
All existing fetch() callers migrated or documented as exceptions
Vitest suite covers 401/403/404/409/422/429/500, requestId, page reset, abort, timeout, stack-trace suppression

newDebt.title: Frontend dependency audit failures are bypassed with `|| true`
newDebt.evidence: dependency-scan job ends with `npm audit --audit-level=high || true`.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — `|| true` removed from npm audit step in dependency-scan job; High/Critical vulnerabilities now fail the job.
acceptanceCriteria: `|| true` removed from npm audit step
Any High/Critical vulnerability fails the job
Vulnerabilities with no fix are registered as debt (not suppressed)

newDebt.title: Flyway validation allows empty or inaccessible migration history
newDebt.evidence: flyway-validation job treats FIRST_COUNT=0 as PASS with a warning.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — flyway-validation job treats FIRST_COUNT=0 as FAIL (no empty-history bypass); SECOND_COUNT verified == FIRST_COUNT.
acceptanceCriteria: FIRST_COUNT=0 causes CI failure
FAILED_COUNT>0 causes CI failure
Connection failure causes CI failure
SECOND_COUNT computed and equals FIRST_COUNT
Latest applied migration version recorded

newDebt.title: Container log-redaction findings do not fail the quality gate
newDebt.evidence: container-smoke job prints LOG_REDACTION: WARN then LOG_REDACTION: PASS in summary.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Closed by Stage 03A — container-smoke job fails on any disallowed secret pattern in logs (no WARN+PASS bypass).
acceptanceCriteria: Any disallowed secret pattern in container logs causes exit 1
Allowlist is narrow (only literal constant phrases)
No environment variable values appear in logs

newDebt.title: Legacy tenantId query parameter retained for API compatibility
newDebt.evidence: Controllers still accept @RequestParam UUID tenantId for backwards API compatibility. JwtAuthenticationFilter rejects mismatches with 403. Ideal end-state is to remove the parameter and use TenantContext exclusively.
acceptanceCriteria: Controllers no longer accept tenantId query parameter
TenantContext is the sole source of tenant scope
API versioning handles the breaking change

newDebt.title: H2 local profile does not enforce RLS
newDebt.evidence: RLS migration V17 is PostgreSQL-only (db/migration-pg-only/). Local profile (H2) relies on application-layer scoping only.
acceptanceCriteria: Local development uses PostgreSQL via Docker (not H2)
RLS enforced in local development

newDebt.title: RLS tenant setting is not automatically bound to application transactions
newDebt.evidence: TenantRlsBinder exists but requires manual invocation from each service. No automatic transaction-level RLS binding via a TenantAwareTransactionManager or equivalent.
newDebt.closureEvidence: TenantRlsBinder + TenantAwareJpaTransactionManager bind RLS tenant setting to the transaction's connection. Verified by TenantTransactionConnectionBindingIntegrationTest (2 tests, 0 failures).
acceptanceCriteria: Automatic RLS binding at transaction start via TenantAwareTransactionManager or equivalent
SET LOCAL executed on the same connection as the transaction
No manual binder calls required from services
Connection-pool leakage test passes (0 leaks)
newDebt.reopenReason: The Stage 04A.2 tests were not executed by the PostgreSQL tenant-isolation job and several tests returned successfully on H2 without exercising RLS.
newDebt.expectedResult: RLS tenant setting is bound to the application transaction's connection
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — TenantRlsBinder binds tenant setting before any RLS-protected query runs

newDebt.title: JWT session validation queries RLS-protected table before tenant context establishment
newDebt.evidence: JwtAuthenticationFilter queries userRepository (RLS-protected) for session_version before TenantContext is established. Under RLS with missing tenant setting, this query returns 0 rows and authentication fails.
newDebt.closureEvidence: JwtSessionValidationServiceImpl establishes provisional TenantContext before querying users table. Verified by validUserA_200 and validUserB_200 (200 OK).
acceptanceCriteria: JwtSessionValidationService established
Provisional verified-token tenant context created before session query
Session query runs inside tenant-bound read-only transaction
TenantAuthenticationUnderRlsIntegrationTest passes on PostgreSQL
newDebt.reopenReason: The Stage 04A.2 tests were not executed by the PostgreSQL tenant-isolation job and several tests returned successfully on H2 without exercising RLS.
newDebt.expectedResult: JWT session validation queries RLS-protected table AFTER TenantContext is established
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — session-version query runs with provisional tenant setting; user found under RLS

newDebt.title: Tenant isolation integration tests run with the local H2 profile
newDebt.evidence: TenantCrudIsolationIntegrationTest and TenantContextLifecycleTest use @ActiveProfiles("local") which uses H2, not PostgreSQL. RLS is not enforced in H2.
newDebt.closureEvidence: RUN_TENANT_POSTGRES_TESTS=true profile runs against PostgreSQL 16 in CI. 71 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 skipped.
acceptanceCriteria: All tenant isolation tests use @ActiveProfiles("prod") or a dedicated PostgreSQL test profile
Tests assert DatabaseMetaData.getDatabaseProductName() == PostgreSQL
No @ActiveProfiles("local") on any test claiming RLS verification
newDebt.reopenReason: The Stage 04A.2 tests were not executed by the PostgreSQL tenant-isolation job and several tests returned successfully on H2 without exercising RLS.
newDebt.expectedResult: Tenant isolation integration tests run against PostgreSQL 16 with real RLS
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — all tenant-isolation tests run under tenant-postgres-test profile with PostgreSQL 16

newDebt.title: Raw PostgreSQL RLS and connection-pool leakage tests are absent
newDebt.evidence: No TenantRlsIntegrationTest (raw JDBC) or TenantConnectionPoolLeakageTest exists. Existing tests only verify application-layer scoping, not database-level RLS.
newDebt.closureEvidence: TenantRlsIntegrationTest (3 tests) verifies RLS at the SQL level. TenantConnectionPoolLeakageTest (1 test) verifies no cross-tenant connection bleed. Both PASS.
acceptanceCriteria: TenantRlsIntegrationTest: raw JDBC, verifies RLS policies, tenant A sees A only, missing context = 0 rows, cross-tenant INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE rejected
TenantConnectionPoolLeakageTest: Hikari pool=1, verifies no context leakage between reused connections
Both tests run against PostgreSQL 16 only
newDebt.reopenReason: The Stage 04A.2 tests were not executed by the PostgreSQL tenant-isolation job and several tests returned successfully on H2 without exercising RLS.
newDebt.expectedResult: Raw PostgreSQL RLS and connection-pool leakage tests exist and pass

newDebt.title: Controllers continue to pass client-supplied tenantId directly to services
newDebt.evidence: 8 controllers accept @RequestParam UUID tenantId and pass it directly to services. JwtAuthenticationFilter rejects mismatches, but the service receives the client value, not the verified TenantContext value.
newDebt.closureEvidence: massAssignment_databaseTenantForcedToA reads the created row via the Fixture DataSource (bypasses RLS) and asserts the stored tenant_id column equals the JWT's tenant (Tenant A), NOT the body-supplied tenantId (Tenant B).
acceptanceCriteria: All controllers use TenantResolver.validateClientSelector() or tenantResolver.requireTenantId()
Services receive verified tenantId from TenantContext, never raw client value
Static gate fails on direct client tenantId passthrough
newDebt.expectedResult: POST with body tenantId=B under tokenA → DB row stored with tenant_id=A, not B
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — Fixture DataSource read confirms stored tenant_id = Tenant A

newDebt.title: TenantContext does not contain a real session identifier or verified capabilities
newDebt.evidence: TenantContext stores email in the sessionId slot. Capabilities set is always empty. JWT has no jti claim.
newDebt.closureEvidence: JwtTokenProvider mints tokens with jti (JWT ID) and session_version claims. JwtSessionValidationServiceImpl compares JWT session_version against the DB users.session_version. Mismatch → 401. Verified by oldSessionVersion_401.
acceptanceCriteria: JWT contains jti (token ID) claim
TenantContext.sessionId populated from jti, not email
TenantContext.sessionVersion populated from JWT session_version claim
Capabilities not recorded as verified when empty
newDebt.reopenReason: The Stage 04A.2 tests were not executed by the PostgreSQL tenant-isolation job and several tests returned successfully on H2 without exercising RLS.
newDebt.expectedResult: TenantContext contains a real session identifier (jti) and session version
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — oldSessionVersion_401 verifies session version mismatch → 401

newDebt.title: Static tenant isolation gate contains warning and counter bypasses
newDebt.evidence: check-tenant-isolation.sh uses [WARN] instead of [FAIL] for DTO mass assignment, subtracts NATIVE_VIOLATIONS from VIOLATIONS counter, and uses file-name-only RLS validation.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Static gate (check_tenant_isolation.py) checks unscoped repository methods, DTO mass assignment, native queries without tenant predicate, RLS policies, test profile consistency. All PASS. No warnings, no counters.
acceptanceCriteria: Static gate rewritten in Python with structured analysis
No [WARN]-only blocks; all violations are [FAIL]
No counter subtraction bypasses
RLS validation checks policy content, not just file name
newDebt.reopenReason: The Stage 04A.2 tests were not executed by the PostgreSQL tenant-isolation job and several tests returned successfully on H2 without exercising RLS.
newDebt.expectedResult: Static tenant isolation gate has no warnings or counters
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — scripts/ci/check_tenant_isolation.py reports PASS with no violations

newDebt.title: Frontend tenant switching and stale-data protection are not implemented or verified
newDebt.evidence: No frontend tenant selector exists. No tests for tenant switch, request cancellation, cache invalidation, or stale-data protection.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Frontend reviewed: no tenant selector exists currently. Status: NOT_APPLICABLE_CURRENTLY (not PASS). No false PASS claimed.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend tenant switching reviewed
If selector exists: tested (switch, abort, cache clear, capability reload)
If selector doesn't exist: documented as NOT_APPLICABLE_CURRENTLY

newDebt.title: Runtime and migration database role separation is not verified
newDebt.evidence: CI uses a single DB role for both Flyway migrations and application runtime. Runtime role may be table owner or superuser, bypassing RLS.
newDebt.closureEvidence: TenantRuntimeRoleIntegrationTest verifies sanad_runtime_app flags. TenantMigrationOwnerForceRlsIntegrationTest verifies FORCE RLS on 8/8 tables. TenantFixtureRoleIntegrationTest verifies fixture role is isolated to CI.
acceptanceCriteria: CI creates sanad_migration_owner and sanad_runtime_app roles
Runtime role: rolsuper=false, rolbypassrls=false, not table owner
V18 migration grants runtime role CRUD privileges
SQL verification in CI: SELECT rolsuper, rolbypassrls FROM pg_roles WHERE rolname = current_user
newDebt.reopenReason: The Stage 04A.2 tests were not executed by the PostgreSQL tenant-isolation job and several tests returned successfully on H2 without exercising RLS.
newDebt.expectedResult: Runtime and migration database role separation is verified
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — sanad_runtime_app (NOSUPERUSER NOBYPASSRLS), sanad_migration_owner (FORCE RLS), sanad_fixture_ci (BYPASSRLS, CI-only)

newDebt.title: FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY removed from tenant-owned production tables
newDebt.evidence: V19 removed FORCE RLS to support fixture inserts. V20 restores it.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Flyway V20 restores FORCE RLS on all 8 tenant-owned tables. TenantMigrationOwnerForceRlsIntegrationTest (3 tests) verifies FORCE RLS is active. CI tenant-isolation job verifies the migration_owner role cannot bypass RLS.
acceptanceCriteria: FORCE RLS restored on all 8 tenant-owned tables (V20)
Runtime role remains non-owner and NOBYPASSRLS
Migration owner is subject to RLS during ordinary DML (FORCE RLS)
Fixture bypass exists only inside ephemeral CI
Mandatory PostgreSQL tenant tests pass with zero skipped
newDebt.verificationCommand: psql -c "SELECT relforcerowsecurity FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'users'"
newDebt.expectedResult: FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY restored on tenant-owned production tables
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — V20 migration restores FORCE RLS; 8/8 tables verified

newDebt.title: Tenant CRUD certification bypasses authentication and authorization
newDebt.evidence: TenantCrudIsolationIntegrationTest uses SecurityPermitAllTestConfig.

Real JWT
Exact status assertions

newDebt.actualResult: PASS — TenantCrudIsolationIntegrationTest runs through real JWT filter chain with RLS

newDebt.title: Authentication-under-RLS test lacks successful and revoked-session scenarios
newDebt.evidence: Only validates JWT claims structure.
newDebt.closureEvidence: TenantAuthenticationUnderRlsIntegrationTest (14 tests) covers: valid auth (200), no auth (401), malformed JWT (401), unknown user (401), suspended user (401), old session version (401), revoked membership (401), inactive membership (401), other-tenant membership (401), archived tenant (401), expired validly-signed JWT (401).
acceptanceCriteria: Valid user → 200
Revoked session → 401
Suspended user → 401
newDebt.expectedResult: Authentication-under-RLS test has successful and revoked-session scenarios
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — validUserA_200 + revokedMembership_401 + oldSessionVersion_401 cover success and revocation

Incremented version → 401
Through MockMvc

newDebt.title: Active tenant membership is not revalidated during JWT session validation
newDebt.evidence: JwtSessionValidationServiceImpl does not query membership table.
newDebt.closureEvidence: JwtSessionValidationServiceImpl queries organization_memberships and requires an ACTIVE membership whose (tenantId, userId) pair exactly matches the JWT claims. 5 scenarios: activeMembership_200, noMembership_401, revokedMembership_401, inactiveMembership_401, membershipInOtherTenant_denied. All PASS.
acceptanceCriteria: Membership query inside session validation
Revoked membership results in 401
Active membership results in authenticated
newDebt.expectedResult: Active tenant membership is revalidated during JWT session validation
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — strict membership entity-match (claims.userId().equals(membership.getUserId())) enforced; 5 scenarios pass

newDebt.title: Expired JWT and archived-tenant denial scenarios are not certified
newDebt.evidence: No expired JWT test or archived-tenant denial test exists.
newDebt.closureEvidence: JwtTokenProvider.mintAccessTokenWithClock uses a fixed Clock anchored 1 hour in the past to mint a token signed by the production key but with exp ~45 min in the past. jjwt rejects it → 401 SANAD-AUTH-001. archivedTenant_denied verifies archived tenant → 401.
acceptanceCriteria: Expired JWT results in 401
Archived tenant results in denied
newDebt.expectedResult: Expired JWT and archived-tenant denial scenarios are certified
newDebt.actualResult: PASS — expiredValidlySignedJwt_401 (real signed token, exp in past) + archivedTenant_denied both PASS

newDebt.title: Application CRUD tenant-isolation certification does not cover resource-level create, read, update and delete operations
newDebt.evidence: CRUD test only covers list and cross-tenant selector.
newDebt.closureEvidence: Organization resource isolation tested directly (not just User resource). GET Org A by id with tokenA → 200. GET Org B by id with tokenA → 404 (cross-tenant denied). CREATE Org in Tenant A with tokenA → 201. All PASS.
acceptanceCriteria: Same-tenant create results in 201
Cross-tenant resource ID results in 404
Mass assignment rejected
newDebt.expectedResult: Application CRUD tenant-isolation certification covers Organization resource

Open blocking P1 = 0

newDebt.actualResult: PASS — check_closure_debt_register.py + check_gitleaks_policy.py enforce consistency in CI

newDebt.title: Lease takeover lacks a fencing token, allowing a stale worker to complete a record after ownership changes.
newDebt.evidence: Lease takeover lacks a fencing token, allowing a stale worker to complete a record after ownership changes. — Stage 05A.2.1 implementation required.

newDebt.title: AuditMigrationUpgradeIntegrationTest does not execute the real V25-to-latest migration path.
newDebt.evidence: AuditMigrationUpgradeIntegrationTest does not execute the real V25-to-latest migration path. — Stage 05A.2.1 implementation required.

newDebt.title: PostgreSQL persistence failures can be masked by broad H2 fallback exception handling.
newDebt.evidence: PostgreSQL persistence failures can be masked by broad H2 fallback exception handling. — Stage 05A.2.1 implementation required.

newDebt.title: Stage-05 RLS certification does not keep transaction-local tenant context and SQL assertions in one transaction.
newDebt.evidence: Stage-05 RLS certification does not keep transaction-local tenant context and SQL assertions in one transaction. — Stage 05A.2.1 implementation required.

newDebt.title: Idempotency interceptor reserves requests but the production endpoint does not invoke IdempotentCommandExecutor, leaving records PROCESSING.
newDebt.evidence: Idempotency interceptor reserves requests but the production endpoint does not invoke IdempotentCommandExecutor, leaving records PROCESSING. — Stage 05A.2.2 implementation required.

newDebt.title: The declared Transaction B relies on self-invocation and calls completion through REQUIRES_NEW, so business, audit and completion are not one transaction.
newDebt.evidence: The declared Transaction B relies on self-invocation and calls completion through REQUIRES_NEW, so business, audit and completion are not one transaction. — Stage 05A.2.2 implementation required.

newDebt.title: Stage-05 runtime-role tests and PostgreSQL stores can resolve the CI fixture DataSource instead of sanad_runtime_app.
newDebt.evidence: Stage-05 runtime-role tests and PostgreSQL stores can resolve the CI fixture DataSource instead of sanad_runtime_app. — Stage 05A.2.2 implementation required.

newDebt.title: Lease ownership can be null and native lease updates are followed by potentially stale JPA entity reads.
newDebt.evidence: Lease ownership can be null and native lease updates are followed by potentially stale JPA entity reads. — Stage 05A.2.2 implementation required.

newDebt.title: Audit hash input is not normalized identically before persistence and verification.
newDebt.evidence: Audit hash input is not normalized identically before persistence and verification. — Stage 05A.2.2 implementation required.

newDebt.title: Runtime database grants include UPDATE and DELETE on immutable audit tables.
